name: Lu Su
description: Lu Su (172–217), courtesy name Zijing, was a military general and strategist serving under the warlord Sun Quan during the late Eastern Han dynasty of China. Renowned for his strategic vision and integrity, Lu Su played a crucial role in the establishment and consolidation of the state of Eastern Wu during the Three Kingdoms period. He initially served under Yuan Shu but later defected to Sun Quan after recognizing his superior leadership. Lu Su succeeded Zhou Yu as the Grand Commander of Eastern Wu's forces following Zhou Yu's death, continuing his predecessor's policy of alliance with Liu Bei against Cao Cao. He strongly advocated for maintaining this alliance, understanding that a divided China was the only way for Eastern Wu to survive. His wisdom and diplomatic skills were instrumental in navigating the complex political landscape of the time, preventing Eastern Wu from being absorbed by the larger, more powerful kingdoms of Wei and Shu. He is remembered as a selfless and insightful strategist, dedicated to the well-being of his lord and his people, and a key figure in shaping the course of the Three Kingdoms era. Unlike some of his contemporaries, Lu Su was known for his generosity and willingness to help those in need, further solidifying his reputation as a virtuous and respected leader. He is often contrasted with the more aggressive and ambitious figures of the era, highlighting his commitment to peace and stability.
subtitle: Wise Strategist of Eastern Wu
achievements: Advising Sun Quan on key strategic decisions

enemies.name: Those who threatened the Sun-Liu alliance

keyMyths.title: Lu Su Succeeds Zhou Yu
keyMyths.description: Following Zhou Yu's death, Lu Su was chosen by Sun Quan to succeed him as Grand Commander. This demonstrated Sun Quan's trust in Lu Su's abilities and commitment to the alliance.

keyMyths.title: Maintaining the Sun-Liu Alliance
keyMyths.description: Lu Su understood the importance of the alliance between Sun Quan and Liu Bei to counter Cao Cao's dominance. He worked tirelessly to preserve the alliance, even in the face of disagreements and betrayals.

keyMyths.title: Lu Su's Generosity
keyMyths.description: Lu Su was known for his generosity and willingness to help those in need. This made him a popular figure among the people and strengthened his position within Eastern Wu.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: The primary historical source for the Three Kingdoms period.
sources.text: The primary historical source for the Three Kingdoms period.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A fictionalized account of the Three Kingdoms period, popularizing many of the key figures and events.
